messages.subject: [R-sig-ins] how to convert a claims development aggregates into the class "triangle" "matrix" with Chain Ladder Package
messages.subject_clean: how to convert a claims development aggregates into the class "triangle" "matrix" with Chain Ladder Package
messages.body_plain:
I have claims development data  that has already aggregate into the
triangles as shown below;

When i read the data into R using the following command:

d<- read.table(file="clipboard",sep="\t",header=TRUE,check.names=FALSE) it
yields a data frame.

However when i check the class of sample data in Chain Ladder

> class(RAA)
[1] "triangle" "matrix"
>
 How can i convert my data to be the same class as RAA


Charles.

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-ins] how to convert a claims development aggregates into the class "triangle" "matrix" with Chain Ladder Package
messages.subject_clean: how to convert a claims development aggregates into the class "triangle" "matrix" with Chain Ladder Package
messages.body_plain:
Hi,

I think you need to use as.triangle and look also to the vignette : http://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/ChainLadder/vignettes/ChainLadder.pdf

CD
---------------------------------------
Christophe Dutang
LMM, UdM, Le Mans, France
web: http://dutangc.free.fr

messages.subject: [R-sig-ins] R in Insurance 2015 Conference Programme
messages.body_plain:
The programme for the 3rd R in Insurance conference is on-line: 
http://ase.uva.nl/news-events/r-in-insurance/programme/programme.html

The event will take place on 29 June 2015 at the University of Amsterdam. 

Time to register now: http://ase.uva.nl/news-events/r-in-insurance/registration-and-fees/registration-and-fees.html

Special thanks to our sponsors, without whom the conference wouldn't be possible: 
CYBAEA, RStudio, APPLIED AI, Milliman, QBE Re, AEGON, Delta Lloyd Amsterdam , Deloitte.

You find impressions from the previous events on http://www.rininsurance.com

We hope to see you in Amsterdam!

----------------------------------------------------------------------
The information in this E-Mail and in any attachments is...{{dropped:19}}
